generic_rules: Repairs should target existing outputs or auxiliary variables in the implementation scope.
Use case-provided bounds and visible feature names when creating range assumptions/guarantees.
Focus repairs on fixing unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Ensure helper declarations have types that match their expressions (bool, int, real).
anti_patterns: Do not introduce AGREE code referencing AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) without corresponding visible ports or constants.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, and owners)
Compact requirement semantics and model traceability
AADL scope rules for annex placement
